---
title: "quarto-diagrams"
subtitle: "Circular diagram styles for Quarto"
toc: false
---

`quarto-diagrams` is a Quarto extension that adds a collection of circular diagram styles to your documents and Reveal.js presentations. Each style shares the same authoring model — a container div with nested `item` divs — but renders differently.

## Installation

```bash
quarto add EmilHvitfeldt/quarto-diagrams
```

Activate the extension in your document YAML:

```yaml
filters:
  - diagrams
```

## Styles

::: {layout-ncol=2}

### [Circle Flow](circle-flow.qmd)

Nodes arranged on a ring, connected by arrows.

::: circle-flow
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
:::

### [Pie](pie.qmd)

Items rendered as equal-sized wedges of a circle.

::: pie
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
::: item
David
:::
::: item
Eve
:::
:::

### [Process](process.qmd)

A linear flow of steps connected by arrows.

::: process
::: item
Alice
:::
::: item
Bob
:::
::: item
Carol
:::
:::

### [Pyramid](pyramid.qmd)

Stacked bands narrowing toward the top.

::: pyramid
::: item
Vision
:::
::: item
Strategy
:::
::: item
Tactics
:::
::: item
Operations
:::
:::

### [Matrix](matrix.qmd)

A 2×2 grid of quadrants along two axes.

::: {.matrix x-axis="Market Share" y-axis="Growth" x-low="Low" x-high="High" y-low="Low" y-high="High"}
::: item
Question Marks
:::
::: item
Stars
:::
::: item
Dogs
:::
::: item
Cash Cows
:::
:::

### [Hierarchy](hierarchy.qmd)

An org chart / tree from nested items.

::::: {.hierarchy}
:::: item
CEO

::: item
CTO
:::

::: item
CFO
:::

::: item
CPO
:::
::::
:::::

### [Venn](venn.qmd)

Two or three overlapping sets.

::: {.venn ab="Shared"}
::: item
Frontend
:::
::: item
Backend
:::
:::

:::

## Shared concepts

Across all styles:

- [Node Shapes](node-shapes.qmd) — circle, box, and text-only nodes
- [Arrow Types](arrow-types.qmd) — chevron, curved, thin, ring, arc, and double arrows
- [Colors](colors.qmd) — global and per-item color control
- [Customization](customization.qmd) — full class and attribute reference
